description: A 60-item measure of the Big Five personality domains: Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, Agreeableness, and Neuroticism, based on the International Personality Item Pool.

consentBlock.title: IPIP-NEO-60 Personality Assessment
consentBlock.bodyText: This assessment contains 60 statements about your personality. For each statement, indicate how accurately it describes you on a scale from Very Inaccurate to Very Accurate. There are no right or wrong answers. Your responses will be used to generate a personality profile as part of your Legacy Portrait. Your data is stored securely and encrypted. This instrument uses items from the International Personality Item Pool (ipip.ori.org), which is in the public domain.
consentBlock.requiredCheckboxLabel: I understand this is a self-report personality assessment and consent to my responses being stored and scored.

disclaimerText: This assessment is for personal insight and legacy-building purposes only. It is not a clinical diagnostic tool. Items are from the public-domain International Personality Item Pool (ipip.ori.org).

openness.text: Your Openness score is low, suggesting you prefer practical, conventional approaches and are comfortable with familiar routines rather than seeking out novel experiences.
Your Openness score is average, indicating a balanced mix of curiosity and practicality. You appreciate new ideas while also valuing tried-and-true methods.
Your Openness score is high, reflecting a strong intellectual curiosity, vivid imagination, and appreciation for art, ideas, and unconventional perspectives.

conscientiousness.text: Your Conscientiousness score is low, suggesting a flexible, spontaneous approach to life. You may prefer to keep your options open rather than follow strict plans.
Your Conscientiousness score is average, indicating a reasonable balance between organization and flexibility in your daily life.
Your Conscientiousness score is high, reflecting strong self-discipline, organization, and a goal-oriented approach to life.

extraversion.text: Your Extraversion score is low, suggesting you prefer quieter, more solitary activities and may find large social gatherings draining.
Your Extraversion score is average, indicating comfort in both social and solitary settings.
Your Extraversion score is high, reflecting an energetic, sociable nature. You thrive in social situations and draw energy from interacting with others.

agreeableness.text: Your Agreeableness score is low, suggesting you tend to be more competitive and skeptical in your interactions with others.
Your Agreeableness score is average, indicating a balance between cooperation and assertiveness.
Your Agreeableness score is high, reflecting a compassionate, cooperative nature. You value harmony and are considerate of others' feelings.

neuroticism.text: Your Neuroticism score is low, suggesting strong emotional stability. You tend to remain calm under pressure.
Your Neuroticism score is average, indicating a typical range of emotional responses to life's challenges.
Your Neuroticism score is high, reflecting a tendency to experience stress and mood fluctuations more intensely.

videoPromptTrigger: Take a moment to reflect on the questions you just answered. Record a brief video sharing your thoughts about how these traits show up in your daily life.

domainDescriptions.openness: Openness to Experience reflects the degree of intellectual curiosity, creativity, and preference for novelty and variety. People high in Openness tend to be imaginative, curious about the world, and appreciative of art and beauty. Those lower in Openness tend to prefer routine, practical thinking, and conventional approaches. Research consistently links Openness to creative achievement, divergent thinking, and engagement with complex ideas.
domainDescriptions.imagination: Imagination captures the tendency to create a rich, vivid inner world of fantasy and mental imagery. High scorers have active imaginations and find that fantasy enriches their lives. Low scorers are more oriented toward facts and concrete reality. Research in personality psychology links this facet to creative potential and the ability to envision novel possibilities.
domainDescriptions.artistic_interests: Artistic Interests reflects appreciation for beauty in art, nature, and aesthetic experiences. High scorers are moved by poetry, music, and visual art, and often seek out cultural experiences. Low scorers tend to be less interested in the arts and more focused on practical matters. This facet is associated with engagement in creative hobbies and cultural participation.
domainDescriptions.conscientiousness: Conscientiousness describes the tendency to be organized, dependable, and disciplined. Highly conscientious people plan ahead, follow through on commitments, and strive for achievement. Those lower in Conscientiousness tend to be more spontaneous and flexible but may struggle with follow-through. Research shows Conscientiousness is one of the strongest personality predictors of job performance, academic success, and health outcomes.
domainDescriptions.self_efficacy: Self-Efficacy reflects confidence in one's ability to accomplish tasks and handle challenges. High scorers believe they have the intelligence and drive to succeed. Low scorers doubt their abilities and may avoid challenging situations. This facet is linked to goal-setting behavior, persistence in the face of obstacles, and overall life satisfaction.
domainDescriptions.orderliness: Orderliness captures the preference for structure, organization, and planning. High scorers keep things tidy, follow schedules, and prefer clear rules. Low scorers are more comfortable with ambiguity and disorder. Research connects this facet to time management skills and the ability to maintain productive routines.
domainDescriptions.extraversion: Extraversion reflects the tendency to seek stimulation in the company of others. Extraverts are energized by social interaction, tend to be enthusiastic and action-oriented, and are often perceived as full of energy. Introverts tend to be more reserved, deliberate, and less dependent on social engagement for energy. Research links Extraversion to positive emotions, social network size, and leadership emergence.
domainDescriptions.friendliness: Friendliness captures warmth and genuine interest in other people. High scorers make friends quickly, are approachable, and enjoy connecting with others. Low scorers are not necessarily hostile but tend to be more reserved and distant in social situations. This facet is associated with the quality and depth of interpersonal relationships.
domainDescriptions.gregariousness: Gregariousness reflects the preference for being around other people and seeking out social gatherings. High scorers enjoy crowds, parties, and group activities. Low scorers prefer solitude or small groups and may find large social events draining. Research links this facet to social network breadth and frequency of social activities.
domainDescriptions.agreeableness: Agreeableness reflects the tendency to be compassionate, cooperative, and trusting toward others. Highly agreeable people value getting along with others, are considerate, and willing to compromise. Those lower in Agreeableness tend to be more competitive, skeptical, and willing to challenge others. Research associates Agreeableness with relationship satisfaction, prosocial behavior, and conflict resolution skills.
domainDescriptions.trust: Trust captures the tendency to believe that others are honest and well-intentioned. High scorers give people the benefit of the doubt and assume good faith. Low scorers tend to be more suspicious and guarded in their interactions. Research shows that interpersonal trust is foundational to building strong relationships and effective teamwork.
domainDescriptions.altruism: Altruism reflects genuine concern for the welfare of others and a willingness to help. High scorers find helping others rewarding and go out of their way to assist people in need. Low scorers may view excessive helpfulness as self-serving or may simply be less motivated by others' needs. This facet is linked to volunteering behavior, charitable giving, and prosocial motivation.
domainDescriptions.neuroticism: Neuroticism reflects the tendency to experience negative emotions such as anxiety, sadness, and irritability. People high in Neuroticism are more emotionally reactive and vulnerable to stress. Those low in Neuroticism tend to be calm, emotionally stable, and resilient under pressure. Research shows Neuroticism is associated with stress reactivity, mental health outcomes, and coping strategies.
domainDescriptions.anxiety: Anxiety captures the tendency to worry, feel nervous, and anticipate problems. High scorers are often apprehensive about the future and may experience tension even in low-threat situations. Low scorers tend to be calm and relaxed. Research links this facet to stress perception, physiological arousal, and the use of avoidant versus approach-oriented coping strategies.
domainDescriptions.self_consciousness: Self-Consciousness reflects sensitivity to what others think and a tendency to feel embarrassed or uncomfortable in social situations. High scorers are easily made uncomfortable by attention and may avoid situations where they could be judged. Low scorers are more socially confident and less affected by others' opinions. This facet is associated with social anxiety and public speaking apprehension.
